description:
Context:
    You are a network engineer verifying basic external and internal reachability from the current Linux host.  
    A plain-text file already exists at  
    /home/user/ip_list.txt  
    containing three IPv4 addresses, one per line.

    Objective:
    1. Read every address in /home/user/ip_list.txt.  
    2. Test its reachability with exactly one ICMP echo request (a single “ping”).  
       • A reply received within one second means the address is reachable (“UP”).  
       • No reply within one second means the address is unreachable (“DOWN”).  
    3. For every address probed, append a single status line to the log file  
       /home/user/connectivity_report.log  
       following the precise format below (no extra spaces, tabs or blank lines):

           IP=<address> STATUS=<UP|DOWN>

       Example of two separate lines (do not copy literally):
           IP=198.51.100.10 STATUS=UP
           IP=192.0.2.1 STATUS=DOWN

    Required behaviour & verification rules:
    • The log file must be created (or overwritten if it already exists) in /home/user.  
    • Lines in the log must appear in the same order as the addresses inside /home/user/ip_list.txt.  
    • Exactly three lines must be present—one for each IP—each terminated with a single UNIX newline (LF).  
    • The automated test will confirm:
        – the file /home/user/connectivity_report.log exists,  
        – it is a regular text file (not a directory, symlink, etc.),  
        – it contains exactly three lines in the format specified,  
        – the reachability status values are correct for the provided test environment.

    Keep the solution to a single compound shell command (a one-liner) or a very small, inline shell script launched directly from the terminal—no external files or complex scripts are required.

    Deliverable for the agent:
    Produce /home/user/connectivity_report.log with correctly formatted reachability results as described above.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:
    • File /home/user/ip_list.txt already exists with the following exact contents (including trailing newline):
          8.8.8.8
          1.1.1.1
          192.0.2.1

    Ground-truth reachability in the execution environment used for grading:
    • 8.8.8.8 responds to ICMP within 1 s  (status “UP”)
    • 1.1.1.1 responds to ICMP within 1 s  (status “UP”)
    • 192.0.2.1 does not respond           (status “DOWN”)

    Thus, after correct execution, /home/user/connectivity_report.log must contain exactly:

        IP=8.8.8.8 STATUS=UP
        IP=1.1.1.1 STATUS=UP
        IP=192.0.2.1 STATUS=DOWN

    Each line ends with a single newline character (LF). No extra blank lines or whitespace.

    File permissions are not critical for verification; default user-created permissions (e.g., 0644) are acceptable.
